summarization.a: The scatter plot shows the composition of a Ge, Sb, and Te film at different pulse lengths of GeCl₄-dioxane.
summarization.b: The scatter plot displays the composition of a Ge, Sb, and Te film at various cycles of GeTe/(GeTe+Sb₂Te₃).

data_extraction.a:
| GeCl₄-dioxane pulse length (s) | Composition (at.%)  Ge|Composition (at.%)  Sb|Composition (at.%)  Te|
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 15 |35|50|
| 2 | 25 |25|50|
| 3 | 30 |20|50|
| 4 | 30 |20|50|
| 6 | 30 |20|50|
data_extraction.b:
| GeTe/(GeTe+Sb₂Te₃) cycling | Composition (at.%)  Ge|Composition (at.%)  Sb|Composition (at.%)  Te|
|---|---|---|---|
| 0.25 | 20 |30|50|
| 0.33 | 22 |28|50|
|0.36|28|22|50|
| 0.5 | 30 |20|50|

a.question: What is the required time to reach Ge saturation?
What material is replaced upon exposure to GeCl2 and how can you tell?
a.answer: a	Sb is replaced with Ge, as for increasing Ge exposure, the Ge percentage increases and the Sb percentage decreases. The Te percentage remains constant.

b.question: For every GeTe cycle, how many Sb2Te3 cycles are there at a fraction of 0.25?
In a supercycle approach, does this method of depicting the cycle ratio make sense when looking into composition data?
b.answer: b	The method to show the cycle ratio can mean 1 to 3 but also 50 to 150, in both cases the composition will be different due to the probing depth of the measurement technique with which the composition is measured.
